description: ECG diagram showing QT interval prolongation due to Class III antiarrhythmics

strategy.thinking: The objective is to find a technical medical diagram that specifically illustrates the electrophysiological effect of Class III antiarrhythmics (potassium channel blockers) on the cardiac cycle, focusing on the prolongation of the QT interval.
strategy.guidance: Select a professional medical diagram that clearly labels the QT interval and demonstrates its extension relative to a standard ECG trace.

reasoning: This image is a professional medical table that clearly categorizes antiarrhythmic drugs by the Vaughan-Williams classification. For Class III drugs (e.g., Amiodarone, Sotalol), it provides a specific ECG diagram that overlays the drug-induced change—a prolonged QT interval, highlighted in blue—onto a standard grey baseline trace. This directly satisfies the requirement to demonstrate the extension relative to a standard trace in a clear, educational format.
selected_image_description: A comprehensive clinical table of antiarrhythmic drug actions. The 'Class III' section lists common agents and includes an ECG diagram showing a normal QRS complex followed by a significantly delayed and prolonged T-wave (highlighted in blue), illustrating the prolongation of the QT interval.
